MRI of the Neck (Cervical Spine)
left neck
pain in your left neck
left shoulder
pain in your left shoulder
for the past two months
An MRI scan was taken of your neck
using different views and techniques to get clear pictures
your neck
The natural curve in your neck looks a bit straighter than usual.
This might be due to how you were positioned during the scan
muscle spasms
spinal discs
levels C4-5 to C6-7
The spaces between your spinal discs from levels C4-5 to C6-7 are moderately to severely narrowed.
the discs themselves are thinner than they should be
signs of wear and tear
bone spurs
the front of your spine in these areas
Your spinal cord
Your spinal cord ... looks normal in size
appears healthy on the MRI
cerebellar tonsils
A part of your brain called the cerebellar tonsils is in its normal position.
harmful growths
your bones
damage to your bones
bone marrow
the joint on the left side of your spine at level C4-5
The bone marrow around the joint on the left side of your spine at level C4-5 shows a particular signal on the MRI.
This likely indicates a reaction to arthritis in that joint.
soft tissues next to your spine
No unusual growths or masses were seen in the soft tissues next to your spine.
C2-3 (Upper Neck)
The discs at this level are normal.
nerve openings
The nerve openings at this level are normal.
spinal canal
There is no narrowing of the spinal canal (C2-3).
a small disc bulge (1-2 mm) in the center
a joint on the right side
mild overgrowth of a joint on the right side (C3-4)
the sac containing your spinal cord
slightly presses on the front of the sac containing your spinal cord (C3-4)
mild narrowing of the spinal canal (C3-4)
The openings for the nerves (C3-4) are not significantly narrowed.
a noticeable disc bulge (C4-5)
bone spurs, especially on the left side (C4-5)
arthritis in the joint on the left side (C4-5)
pressure on the front of the sac around your spinal cord (C4-5)
mild narrowing of the spinal canal (C4-5)
The openings for the nerves (C4-5) are moderately narrowed on the right
The openings for the nerves (C4-5) are moderately to severely narrowed on the left.
a noticeable disc bulge (C5-6)
bone spurs, especially on the right side (C5-6)
the joints on both sides
mild overgrowth of the joints on both sides (C5-6)
pressure on the front of the sac around your spinal cord (C5-6)
mild narrowing of the spinal canal (C5-6)
The openings for the nerves (C5-6) are moderately to severely narrowed on the right
The openings for the nerves (C5-6) are mildly narrowed on the left.
a noticeable disc bulge (C6-7)
bone spurs (C6-7)
pressure on the front of the sac around your spinal cord (C6-7)
mild narrowing of the spinal canal (C6-7)
The openings for the nerves (C6-7) are moderately to severely narrowed on both the right
The openings for the nerves (C6-7) are moderately to severely narrowed on the left sides.
C7-T1 (Lower Neck/Upper Back)
The discs at this level (C7-T1) are normal.
The nerve openings at this level (C7-T1) are normal.
There is no narrowing of the spinal canal (C7-T1).
several levels of your neck spine
signs of wear and tear (degenerative disease) at several levels of your neck spine
small joints at several levels of your neck spine
arthritis in the small joints at several levels of your neck spine
These changes are most significant between levels C4-5, C5-6, and C6-7.

source_propositions.text: NationalRad Sample Neuroradiology Report
PATIENT: JOHN SMITH
PHYSICIAN: REFERRING
EXAM: MRI CERVICAL SPINE
Left neck and shoulder pain for 2 months
source_propositions.normalized: left neck pain and shoulder pain, 2 months duration

source_propositions.text: No comparison studies were performed.
No contrast was given.
MRI of the cervical spine was obtained.
The following sequences were used: Axial GRE, sagittal T1, T2, and STIR.
source_propositions.normalized: mri sequences: axial gre, sagittal t1, t2, stir

source_propositions.text: There is straightening of the normal cervical lordosis.
The straightening of cervical lordosis may be positional.
The straightening of cervical lordosis may be related to muscular spasm.
source_propositions.normalized: cervical lordosis straightening muscular spasm cause

source_propositions.text: There is moderate to severe disc space narrowing from C4-5 through C6-7.
source_propositions.normalized: moderate to severe disc space narrowing c4-c5 to c6-c7

source_propositions.text: There is prominent prevertebral spondylosis.
Spinal cord is normal in caliber.
Spinal cord is normal in signal intensity.
Cerebellar tonsils are normally located.
No destructive bony lesions.
There is low T1 signal intensity within the bone marrow surrounding the left C4-5 facet joint.
source_propositions.normalized: low t1 signal intensity in bone marrow surrounding left c4-c5 facet joint

source_propositions.text: The low T1 signal intensity within the bone marrow may be reactive to facet arthropathy.
source_propositions.normalized: low t1 signal intensity in bone marrow possibly reactive to facet arthropathy

source_propositions.text: At C2-3, no disc osteophyte complex.
At C2-3, no spinal canal stenosis.
At C2-3, no neural foraminal narrowing.
At C3-4, there is a focal 1-2 mm central disc protrusion.
source_propositions.normalized: c3-c4 focal central disc protrusion, 1-2 mm

source_propositions.text: At C3-4, there is mild right uncovertebral joint hypertrophy.
source_propositions.normalized: c3-c4 mild right uncovertebral joint hypertrophy

source_propositions.text: At C3-4, there is deformity of the ventral thecal sac.
At C3-4, there is a mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C3-4, no significant neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: no c3-c4 significant neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C4-5, there is a diffuse disc osteophyte more prominent on the left.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 diffuse disc osteophyte, prominent on left

source_propositions.text: At C4-5, there is left facet arthropathy.
At C4-5, there is flattening of the ventral thecal sac.
At C4-5, there is a mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C4-5, there is moderate right neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 moderate right neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C4-5, there is moderate to severe left neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 moderate to severe left neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C5-6, there is a diffuse disc osteophyte more prominent on the right.
source_propositions.normalized: c5-c6 diffuse disc osteophyte, prominent on right

source_propositions.text: At C5-6, there is mild bilateral facet joint hypertrophy.
source_propositions.normalized: c5-c6 mild bilateral facet joint hypertrophy

source_propositions.text: At C5-6, there is flattening of the ventral thecal sac.
At C5-6, there is a mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C5-6, there is moderate to severe right neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c5-c6 moderate to severe right neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C5-6, there is mild left neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c5-c6 mild left neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C6-7, there is a diffuse disc osteophyte.
At C6-7, there is flattening of the ventral thecal sac.
At C6-7, there is a mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C6-7, there is moderate to severe bilateral neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c6-c7 moderate to severe bilateral neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C7-T1, no disc osteophyte complex.
At C7-T1, no spinal canal stenosis.
At C7-T1, no neural foraminal narrowing.
No paraspinal soft tissue mass.
There is multilevel degenerative disease.
There is multilevel facet arthropathy.
The degenerative disease and facet arthropathy are most severe from C4-5 through C6-7.
source_propositions.normalized: degenerative disease and facet arthropathy most severe c4-c5 to c6-c7

source_propositions.text: At C6-7, there is mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C6-7, there is flattening of the ventral thecal sac.
At C6-7, there is moderate to severe bilateral neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c6-c7 moderate to severe bilateral neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C5-6, there is mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C5-6, there is flattening of the ventral thecal sac.
At C5-6, there is moderate to severe right neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c5-c6 moderate to severe right neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C5-6, there is mild left neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c5-c6 mild left neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C4-5, there is mild spinal canal stenosis.
At C4-5, there is flattening of the ventral thecal sac.
At C4-5, there is moderate right neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 moderate right neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C4-5, there is moderate to severe left neural foraminal narrowing.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 moderate to severe left neural foraminal narrowing

source_propositions.text: At C4-5, there is left facet arthropathy.
At C4-5, there is surrounding low T1 signal intensity within the bone marrow.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 low t1 signal intensity in surrounding bone marrow

source_propositions.text: The low T1 signal intensity within the bone marrow at C4-5 is likely reactive.
source_propositions.normalized: c4-c5 low t1 bone marrow signal intensity likely reactive

source_propositions.text: The report was approved by a Board Certified Radiologist.
source_propositions.normalized: report approved by board certified radiologist

source_propositions.text: This report was electronically signed.
